content:
First note that if \(\deg f=n\), then the left side
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)
\]
has degree \(n\), while the right side
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
\]
has degree \(2n-1\).  Equating degrees \(n=2n-1\) forces \(n=1\).  So write
\[
f(x)=ax+b,\qquad a\neq0.
\]
Then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)
=(a(x-1)+b)+(ax+b)+(a(x+1)+b)
=3ax+3b,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{(ax+b)^2}{2013\,x}
=\frac{a^2x^2+2abx+b^2}{2013\,x}
=\frac{a^2x^2}{2013x}+\frac{2abx}{2013x}+\frac{b^2}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2}{2013}x+\frac{2ab}{2013}+\frac{b^2}{2013x}.
\]
For this to be a genuine polynomial (and equal to \(3ax+3b\)), the \(1/x\)‐term must vanish, so \(b^2=0\), hence \(b=0\).  Then matching coefficients of \(x\) and constants gives
\[
\frac{a^2}{2013}=3a,\quad \frac{2ab}{2013}=3b=0.
\]
Since \(b=0\), the second is automatic, and \(a\neq0\) forces
\[
a^2=3\cdot2013\,a\quad\Longrightarrow\quad a=2013\cdot3=6039.
\]
Thus the only nonconstant solution is
\[
f(x)=6039x,
\]
and hence \(f(1)=6039\).  The sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is
\[
\boxed{6039}.
\]

=3ax+3b,
\]

\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{(ax+b)^2}{2013\,x}
=\frac{a^2x^2+2abx+b^2}{2013\,x}
=\frac{a^2x^2}{2013x}+\frac{2abx}{2013x}+\frac{b^2}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2}{2013}x+\frac{2ab}{2013}+\frac{b^2}{2013x}.

For this to be a genuine polynomial (and equal to \(3ax+3b\)), the \(1/x\)‐term must vanish, so \(b^2=0\), hence \(b=0\).  Then matching coefficients of \(x\) and constants gives
